chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي صُنُوفِ أَهْلِ الْخِلَافِ وَ ذِكْرِ الْقَدَرِيَّةِ وَ الْخَوَارِجِ وَ الْمُرْجِئَةِ وَ أَهْلِ الْبُلْدَانِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 175 – Regarding the types of the adversaries, and mention of the Qadiriyya, and the Kharijites, and the Murjiites, and the people of the cities

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/220)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/220)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَهْلُ الشَّامِ شَرٌّ أَمْ أَهْلُ الرُّومِ فَقَالَ إِنَّ الرُّومَ كَفَرُوا وَ لَمْ يُعَادُونَا وَ إِنَّ أَهْلَ الشَّامِ كَفَرُوا وَ عَادَوْنَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The people of Syria are more evil than the people of Rome?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Romans disbelieved and they were not inimical to us<sup>asws</sup>, but the people of Syria disbelieved and were inimical to us<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn Sa’id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from Sayf ibn ‘Amirah from abu Bakr al-Hadrami who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘Are people of Damascus more evil than the people of Rome?’ The Imam said, ‘People of Rome disbelieved, but are not hostile to us. The people of Damascus have disbelieved and are hostile to us.’”
